methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rainmaker operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector at Series A stage, having raised $25M to date. The broader comp universe consists of 8 private companies in the sector, including Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No direct public comparables exist in this category.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to data constraints. The Stage-Progression approach applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $25M total raised as a proxy for current valuation, implying a $50M post-money valuation at Series A completion. A Peer-Median approach was not viable; while five same-sector peers exist, the filtered set (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier) yielded fewer than three datapoints, making median comparison unreliable and potentially misleading.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, with a median of $50M. This triangulation is based exclusively on stage-progression mechanics and carries material uncertainty. The primary constraint is the narrow peer comparable set—no same-stage or tightly comparable private companies with sufficient valuation data are available for cross-validation. Investors should weight this valuation as preliminary and condition any final assessment on deeper diligence into unit economics, addressable market sizing, and competitive positioning relative to the identified peer group.
